payload.text:
Create a premium, high-end, black and white website for a lifestyle and clothing brand called IM — I AM.

This is not just apparel.
It is a mindset movement built on discipline, identity, and alignment.

The entire website must feel:

• Minimal
• Bold
• Cinematic
• Powerful
• Masculine but universal
• Aspirational yet grounded
• Luxury streetwear aesthetic
• Black & white only (no color except subtle grayscale tones)

No bright colors. No clutter. No playful fonts.
Use strong typography. Clean layouts. Sharp contrast.
Whitespace should feel intentional and premium.

🧠 BRAND MESSAGE TO EMBED THROUGHOUT:

Core Philosophy:

“I AM already everything I want to be. I’m living the lifestyle, putting in the work, and the world naturally aligns with my discipline, hustle, and consistency.”

This brand is NOT future-focused.
It does not chase.
It maintains identity.

Key emotional themes:
• Discipline
• Certainty
• Alignment
• Consistency
• Self-respect
• Ownership
• Lifestyle > Motivation

Tagline:
IM — A LIFESTYLE, NOT A LABEL

🏠 WEBSITE STRUCTURE
1️⃣ HERO SECTION (Full Screen)

Black background.
White bold typography.

Large headline:

"I’M EVERYTHING I WANT TO BE."

Subtext:
"You’re not becoming. You’re maintaining who you already are."

Minimal call to action buttons:
• Shop The Collection
• Discover The Mindset

Background:
Cinematic black & white lifestyle imagery — training, focus, solitude, discipline.

2️⃣ ABOUT THE MINDSET SECTION

Headline:
"This Is Not Motivation."

Text should communicate:
• Live as the person you want to be — now
• Discipline is the bridge while the world aligns
• Most people quit before life adjusts
• I AM exists to shift perspective

Typography should feel powerful and spaced out.

3️⃣ OFFICIAL BRAND STORY SECTION

Make this cinematic and emotional.

Include the origin story:

• Small town in Bulgaria (5,000 people)
• Poverty as a belief system
• One-room apartment
• Designed logo alone
• Car crash moment of awakening
• Disappeared into discipline
• Training, nutrition, self-education
• Left comfort
• The world aligned

Style this like a manifesto — broken paragraphs, dramatic spacing.

4️⃣ PRODUCT SECTION

Minimal grid layout.
Black and white product photography.
High contrast lighting.

Each product page should emphasize:
“This isn’t something you wear to feel motivated.
It represents who you already are.”

5️⃣ SOCIAL IMPACT SECTION

Headline:
"Discipline With Purpose."

Text:

IM donates 25% of profits to disabled children in Turkey and Bulgaria.

This section should feel sincere, not promotional.
Minimal icons.
Black background, white typography.

6️⃣ CLOSING STATEMENT

Large centered text:

"Life aligns when you do."

Subtext:
IM — A Lifestyle, Not a Label.

🎨 DESIGN RULES

• Strict black & white color palette
• Modern sans-serif fonts
• Heavy font weights for headlines
• Smooth scroll animations
• Fade-in text transitions
• Luxury spacing
• No stock-feeling design
• No generic corporate vibe
• It must feel like a movement

**Defining the Brand Essence**

I'm now zeroing in on the core of the "IM — I AM" brand. The focus is on translating its premium, high-end, black and white, minimal, and bold aesthetic into concrete design choices. I'm visualizing a cinematic, powerful feel for the brand's visual identity, starting with the mood board.


**Refining the Brand Identity**

I've been drilling down on the "I AM" brand's philosophy, solidifying the idea that it's about present-state maintenance, rather than future aspirations. The palette is locked at strict black and white with a few grays. I am finalizing the website sections: Hero, Mindset, Brand Story, Product, Social Impact, and Closing. I'm imagining a cinematic narrative inspired by its origin.


**Outlining the Technical Build**

I'm now outlining the tech stack. I'll use reusable UI components (Navbar, Buttons, ProductCard), page-specific sections, and constants for product data. For styling, I'll leverage Tailwind's utilities like `bg-black`, `text-white`, `grayscale`, `tracking-widest`, and large padding. I'll implement fade-in animations with CSS transitions and `IntersectionObserver`.



**Constructing the Technical Design**

I'm now deep-diving into the technical implementation. I'm focusing on creating a `useIntersectionObserver` hook for scroll animations and defining reusable components like the `Navbar` (thinking minimal with a glass effect or solid black), and `Button` (white with black text or outlined). I'm also planning the structure of different sections, from the `Hero` (full viewport image) to the `Footer` (closing statement), keeping the brand's aesthetic in mind. I'll store the provided copy in a `constants.ts` file. Finally, I'm thinking about the right font: it needs to feel like luxury streetwear.


**Visualizing the Placeholder**

I'm now thinking about the placeholders, and planning to generate images with a `grayscale` filter. For scroll reveal animations, I'll leverage a simplified `useIntersectionObserver` hook and apply transitions based on visibility. I'm going for that moody, high-end feel for the placeholder images.

I'm also exploring a strategy for the sans font by forcing uppercase and using tight or wide tracking, and also updating the story section to look like a manifesto with a jagged edge.


**Implementing the Components**

I'm now building out the technical pieces. I've added Tailwind through CDN in `index.html`. In `App.tsx`, I'm orchestrating the sections. I'm focusing on the `FadeIn` component for consistent animation. The `Hero` will require a striking image, ensuring it's dark enough for white text. The story is on track; I'll ensure the Bulgaria/Car Crash text is prominent. Lastly, I'm verifying the work against the requirements.
